Account agreement, Reg DD, and signature card are pre-populated with both signers' data (Marcus's from profile, Adaeze's from the submission).

beats.head: Maestro generates account numbers and routes identity proofing.
beats.lede: New account numbers are generated and staged (not yet active). Adaeze's identity-verification link is queued for SMS/email delivery. She'll be routed through CLEAR to establish her identity and confirm her role as a joint account holder.

beats.head: Maestro pre-fills E-Sign consent and disclosure documents.
beats.lede: Reg DD, Truth in Savings, and E-Sign consent forms are pre-filled with both signers' names, account details, and product features. Tone is plain-language and mobile-optimized — no jargon, no 8-page PDFs to scroll through.

beats.head: Maestro stages signature card and initial deposit.
beats.lede: Signature card shows both signers and joint authority language. Initial $5,000 deposit from Marcus's savings is staged but not yet settled — it will post when both signers' identities are verified and documents are executed.

beats.head: Account documents ready for dual signature and settlement.
beats.lede: All account-opening documents are staged. Adaeze's identity-verification SMS lands within 10 minutes. Marcus and Adaeze will both sign the account docs in one ceremony, and the $5,000 initial deposit will settle within 2 hours of the second signature.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 4 · ENVELOPE DELIVERED
scenes.head: Marcus and Adaeze sign the joint account documents in one ceremony on mobile.
scenes.lede:
Once Adaeze completes identity verification (CLEAR), Maestro routes the account-opening envelope to both signers. Marcus and Adaeze sign all documents in one flow on their respective phones. Accounts open and the initial deposit settles same day.

No branch visit, no notary, no mailed forms — full digital account opening.

beats.head: Adaeze completes CLEAR identity verification.
beats.lede: Adaeze receives the SMS/email with her identity-verification link. She opens CLEAR inline in the app. Government ID scan, liveness check, biometric match — 90 seconds. OFAC/PEP screens run silently. Adaeze is verified.

beats.head: Both signers receive the account-opening envelope.
beats.lede: Maestro routes the envelope to Marcus and Adaeze via app notification/SMS. One envelope, two signers, four documents (Joint Account Agreement, Reg DD, E-Sign Consent, Signature Card). Each opens it on their phone.

beats.head: Marcus and Adaeze review the account details.
beats.lede: Both receive the same view: joint checking and HYSA accounts, initial deposit ($5,000), account numbers (staged), product features. Marcus goes first; Adaeze follows. Both confirm the details and move to signing.

beats.head: Both signers adopt one signature for all four documents.
beats.lede: Single signature adopted for each signer and applied across Account Agreement, Reg DD, E-Sign Consent, and Signature Card. The guided flow walks both signers through each document in sequence. One e-signature style per signer, one audit trail across all four.

beats.head: Accounts open and initial deposit settles same day.
beats.lede: The moment Adaeze's signature clears, Cedar's core banking system books the accounts, assigns account numbers (previously staged), and settles the $5,000 initial deposit from Marcus's existing savings. Both accounts are live within 2 hours of the second signature. Marcus and Adaeze receive account confirmation, debit cards are ordered for overnight delivery, and statements are queued for first-month delivery.

scenes.tag: SCENE 4 OF 4 · WORKSPACE TAIL · ONGOING RELATIONSHIP
scenes.head: Cedar Federal and the Williams household continue in one shared Workspace.
scenes.lede:
The household Workspace goes live the moment accounts are open and funded. Marcus and Adaeze receive access to account statements, transfer history, and upcoming tasks (debit card delivery, first statement). Cedar's relationship team has the same view — no duplicate files, no conflicting versions.

Relationship moves from account opening to ongoing servicing without a handoff.

beats.head: Marcus and Adaeze access their household Workspace.
beats.lede: Cedar-branded email invites both into their household Workspace. Summary shows: joint checking (opened today, $5,000 balance), joint HYSA (opened today, $0 balance, 0.05% APY). Debit cards ordered (delivery expected tomorrow).

beats.head: Account status and tasks in one shared view.
beats.lede: Overview shows account balances, recent activity, and account features (no fees, APY rates). Tasks show debit-card delivery pending and first statement expected (30 days). Both signers see the same state — no surprises, no separate portals.

beats.head: Iris tracks the household in the agent rail.
beats.lede: Activity shows every account-opening step completed, initial deposit settled, and card-delivery status. Ask Iris answers questions about account features, fee structures, transfer limits, and interest accrual without Marcus or Adaeze re-explaining their household structure.

beats.head: Annual Reg DD disclosure refresh task open.
beats.lede: When annual renewal comes due, the task is a click-to-agree on the updated Reg DD disclosure — no new static form to sign. Marcus or Adaeze accepts, Cedar's compliance log updates, audit trail captured.

beats.head: Account relationship in motion.
beats.lede: Joint accounts active, household in the same Workspace, all documents and transactional history tracked. Cedar's compliance and relationship teams have the complete digital file from account opening through ongoing servicing. Marcus and Adaeze never wonder about their account status or renewal deadlines — the Workspace shows what's current and what's coming.

auth-fabric.subtitle: × Holcomb Family — One-Time IRA Rollover-In Initiation

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · CLEAR AT THE DOOR
scenes.head: Walter logs into Cypress's client portal to initiate an IRA rollover.
scenes.lede:
Walter Holcomb, account owner at the Holcomb family's wealth relationship, opens Cypress Wealth's client portal to initiate a one-time IRA rollover-in from his former employer's 401(k) — $1.4M transfer.

Maestro intercepts the portal login and invokes CLEAR. Biometric proof of the account owner before any high-value investment instruction is authorized.

beats.head: Walter opens the Cypress client portal.
beats.lede: Cypress Wealth client portal. Walter is a recognized account owner with existing IRA and joint taxable accounts on file.

beats.head: Maestro intercepts the login and invokes CLEAR.
beats.lede: One tap. Maestro routes Walter to CLEAR — inline, no separate two-factor-authentication step. The portal recognizes him as an existing client and streamlines to biometric verification.

beats.head: Biometric verification on Walter's phone.
beats.lede: CLEAR session opens within the portal. Facial biometric scan against the identity he enrolled during account opening — government ID re-verification optional for repeat trusted clients. 90 seconds.

beats.head: Biometric match returns clean.
beats.lede: CLEAR confirms Walter's identity against his enrolled biometric. No anomalies flagged. KYC-bound (Walter's identity now re-verified against his IRA account record). Session-level proof of account owner identity established — witness event #1 logged.

beats.head: Authenticated session opens; Walter sees his portfolio dashboard.
beats.lede: Walter is back in the portal with biometric proof of identity bound to this session. His existing accounts are visible — IRA balance, joint taxable accounts, positions. No second verification required for the rollover instruction.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 3 · IN-SESSION ACTION · MAESTRO SERVES WEBFORM
scenes.head: Walter initiates the rollover instruction inside the authenticated session.
scenes.lede:
Walter clicks "Initiate Rollover" on his portfolio dashboard. Maestro serves a dynamic rollover-instruction webform inline — pre-filled with his IRA information and ready to capture the source institution details for the $1.4M 401(k) rollover-in.

No second IDV. The biometric proof from Scene 1 is inherited by this transaction.

beats.head: Walter clicks "Initiate Rollover" on his portfolio dashboard.
beats.lede: Walter has a pending 401(k) rollover from his former employer (AT&T). The amount is $1.4M. He wants to roll it into his existing Cypress IRA to consolidate his retirement assets.

beats.head: Maestro serves the rollover-instruction webform inline.
beats.lede: Form opens in the portal. Destination IRA (Cypress Traditional IRA) is pre-selected. Rollover source (AT&T 401(k)), amount ($1.4M), and receiving instructions are ready for entry. Walter confirms his former employer's plan details and authorizes Cypress to request the distribution.

beats.head: Walter confirms the rollover structure and trustee-to-trustee instructions.
beats.lede: Rollover is structured as trustee-to-trustee (no interim distribution to Walter, no tax withholding). Cypress's custodian instructions are pre-filled for AT&T's plan administrator to wire the funds directly to the Cypress IRA custodial account. Settlement expected within 5-10 business days.

beats.head: Iris pre-screens the rollover for compliance and IRA-limit violations.
beats.lede: In the background, Iris checks the transaction — IRA contribution limits are clear (rollover is not subject to annual contribution caps), trustee-to-trustee mechanism avoids 60-day issues, and destination IRA qualifies to receive 401(k) rollovers. The rollover is compliant.

beats.head: Walter submits the rollover instruction.
beats.lede: One click submits the form. The instruction is bound to Walter's biometric proof from the session login — witness event #2 logged. The rollover instruction is queued for Cypress's operations team to contact AT&T's plan administrator and request the distribution.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 3 · CHAIN OF CUSTODY · AGREEMENT DESK VISUALIZES MAESTRO'S BINDING
scenes.head: Agreement Desk surfaces the auth event and the rollover-instruction event linked as one continuous identity thread.
scenes.lede:
Cypress's IRA Operations team reviews the rollover instruction in the Agreement Desk. Maestro has been binding the auth event to the rollover-instruction event in the orchestration layer the whole time — Agreement Desk is the on-screen proxy that makes that invisible work visible.

Identity + transaction linked. Chain-of-custody gap closed. Rollover instruction approved for plan-administrator outreach.

beats.head: Operations opens the Holcomb rollover instruction in Agreement Desk.
beats.lede: The desk shows the new rollover instruction with linked identity context. Session biometric (Walter, time 15:18) connected to the rollover-instruction submission (15:25).

beats.head: Agreement Desk shows the identity thread.
beats.lede: Two events visible in a single timeline: "Verified by CLEAR · Walter Holcomb · Biometric match · Account confirmed · 15:18" → "Rollover Instruction · AT&T 401(k) → IRA · $1.4M · Trustee-to-Trustee · 15:25".

beats.head: Iris confirms the rollover is compliant with IRA and plan rules.
beats.lede: 401(k) rollovers are permitted into IRAs. Trustee-to-trustee mechanism avoids 60-day pitfalls. Annual contribution limits don't apply to rollovers. Cypress's IRA custodian is qualified to receive. Biometric binding confirms Walter as the authorized account owner.

beats.head: Operations approves and contacts AT&T's plan administrator.
beats.lede: One click in Agreement Desk marks the instruction as approved. Cypress's operations team is queued to contact AT&T's plan administrator with the rollover request. Distribution is expected within 5-10 business days.

beats.head: Rollover instruction processed; funds expected within 5-10 days; full audit trail retained.
beats.lede: Cypress's operations team contacts AT&T's plan administrator. AT&T initiates the trustee-to-trustee distribution of $1.4M to Cypress's IRA custodial account (routing and account details provided by Cypress). Funds are expected to settle within 5-10 business days. Walter's IRA balance will reflect the $1.4M addition when the wire clears. The complete chain — portal login (CLEAR) → rollover instruction (Maestro webform) → Operations approval → plan-administrator outreach — is documented as a continuous identity thread. Regulators and the IRA custodian see the defensible record of Walter's biometric identity binding him to this rollover instruction.

intake.subtitle: × Holcomb Family — Discovery-Config Webform for Multi-Account IRA + Family Trust

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 4 · INTAKE · SENDER WEBFORM
scenes.head: Reggie submits a discovery-config webform to set up the Holcomb family's multi-account portfolio.
scenes.lede:
Reggie Adesanya, Senior Financial Advisor at Cypress Wealth, opens the discovery intake form to configure the Holcomb family's multi-account portfolio — IRA rollover, joint taxable account, and new family trust.

The adaptive webform captures household structure, goals, and account intent. Maestro fans out customized account documents and trust-establishment papers the moment Reggie submits.

beats.head: Reggie opens the discovery-config webform.
beats.lede: Cypress's Agreement Desk shows the "New Household Discovery" webform. Reggie opens a new engagement for the Holcomb family (first-time clients). He enters: household members (Walter, age 65, retiring; Esther, age 62, retiring), household state of domicile (Florida).

beats.head: Account intent and structure selections drive the package.
beats.lede: Reggie selects account types: IRA (rollover-in from Walter's 401(k), $1.4M), Joint Taxable (Walter + Esther, multi-asset, intended for growth), Family Trust (grantors: Walter + Esther; beneficiaries: adult children + grandchildren via trust vehicle). Multi-account scope recognized.

beats.head: Goals and risk profile captured.
beats.lede: Reggie interviews via tablet (or phone): retirement income goals ($120k/year), longevity horizon (both to age 95), risk tolerance (moderate, some growth, meaningful income), charitable intent (legacy goal). Inputs are captured in the discovery form.

beats.head: Reggie selects advisory agreement and fiduciary-service tier.
beats.lede: Account-based advisory fee: 0.75% on AUM. Fiduciary tier: full fiduciary for IRA and Joint Taxable; trustee services for the Family Trust. Trust entity state: Florida. Reggie confirms and submits.

beats.head: Maestro submission routes to account documents and trust-establishment package.
beats.lede: Reggie clicks Submit. Maestro pulls the discovery data and generates: IRA Rollover Agreement, Joint Account Agreement, Family Trust Establishment Documents, Advisory Agreement (tailored to fiduciary tier), Suitability Acknowledgment, and ADV-Part-2 Delivery. All are pre-filled from the discovery data and queued for review and execution.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 4 · MAESTRO PACKAGES
scenes.head: Maestro generates six account-opening and trust-establishment documents with multi-party routing in parallel.
scenes.lede:
Maestro pulls account templates and trust-establishment language, pre-populates from the discovery data. IRA Rollover Agreement, Joint Account Agreement, Family Trust Establishment, Advisory Agreement (fiduciary), Suitability, and ADV delivery are drafted and staged. Trust entity formation papers are queued. Compliance review is triggered.

What used to be a 2-3 week document-generation and multi-party review process is now a same-day package.

beats.head: Maestro pulls the IRA Rollover Agreement template.
beats.lede: Template recognizes: Walter as account owner, rollover source (AT&T 401(k), $1.4M), destination (Cypress Traditional IRA), beneficiary (Esther, contingent to adult children via trust). Agreement terms auto-populate from the advisory tier selected (0.75% fee).

beats.head: Maestro generates the Joint Account Agreement.
beats.lede: Joint Account Agreement covers: Walter and Esther as co-owners, account purpose (taxable multi-asset), fee structure (0.75% AUM), and advisory scope (full fiduciary). Account number is staged but not yet active.

beats.head: Maestro drafts the Family Trust Establishment documents.
beats.lede: Trust documents (Florida law) establish: grantors (Walter + Esther), trustees (Walter + Esther initially; successor trustees named), beneficiaries (adult children + grandchildren), and distribution schedule (income to grantors during lifetime; principal to heirs at death). Trust entity formation papers are queued for state filing.

beats.head: Maestro generates the Advisory Agreement (fiduciary version).
beats.lede: Advisory Agreement reflects: fiduciary duty for IRA and Joint Taxable, trustee services for the Family Trust, fee structure (0.75% AUM), performance reporting, and rebalancing cadence (quarterly). All terms are pre-filled from the discovery data.

beats.head: Maestro generates Suitability Acknowledgment and ADV delivery.
beats.lede: Suitability Acknowledgment references the discovery risk profile (moderate, income + growth, 30-year horizon) and confirms the recommended asset allocation. ADV-Part-2 is staged for delivery to both Walter and Esther (required for fiduciary relationships).

beats.head: Package ready for compliance review and dual-signer execution.
beats.lede: All six documents generated, pre-filled, and staged for compliance review. Cypress's compliance officer will review for fiduciary compliance, fee transparency, and trust-entity formation. Once approved, the package routes to Walter and Esther for execution.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 4 · ENVELOPE DELIVERED
scenes.head: Walter and Esther sign all six account and trust documents in one ceremony.
scenes.lede:
Once compliance approves, Maestro routes the envelope to Walter and Esther. Identity verification (CLEAR) routes with the envelope. Both signers authenticate and sign all six documents in one flow. Multi-account relationship opens and trust entity is established same day.

What used to take three separate appointments and a notary visit is now one digital ceremony.

beats.head: Cypress-branded email routes the complete package to Walter and Esther.
beats.lede: One email, one link, six documents. Walter and Esther receive: IRA Rollover Agreement, Joint Account Agreement, Family Trust Establishment, Advisory Agreement, Suitability Acknowledgment, and ADV-Part-2 Delivery — all pre-populated and ready for review.

beats.head: CLEAR routes before signing for both signers.
beats.lede: Walter and Esther must verify before any documents execute. CLEAR fires for each — government ID, liveness, biometric match. Both are verified and linked to the trust-establishment documents that name them as grantors and trustees. No separate entity-verification call required.

beats.head: Walter and Esther review the complete package.
beats.lede: Both receive the same view: six documents, multi-account structure, fiduciary relationship, trust entity, fee schedule (0.75% AUM), and performance reporting cadence. Walter reviews the IRA and Joint Account first; Esther confirms. Both then review the Family Trust and Advisory Agreement together.

beats.head: Both Holcombs sign all six documents.
beats.lede: Single signature adopted for each signer and applied across all six: IRA Rollover, Joint Account, Family Trust, Advisory, Suitability, and ADV Delivery. The guided flow walks both through each in sequence. One e-signature style per signer, one audit trail across all six.

beats.head: Package routes to trust entity formation and account booking.
beats.lede: The moment Esther's signature clears, Cypress's operations team books all three accounts (IRA, Joint Taxable, Trust), generates account numbers, and initiates trust entity formation with the Florida Secretary of State. Walter's $1.4M rollover instruction is activated. The trust documents are filed with the local courthouse as needed. All three account relationships and the trust entity are live within 2 hours of the second signature.

scenes.tag: SCENE 4 OF 4 · WORKSPACE TAIL · ONGOING RELATIONSHIP
scenes.head: Cypress, the Holcombs, and the named co-trustee continue in one shared Workspace.
scenes.lede:
The household Workspace goes live the moment all documents are executed. Walter and Esther receive access to all three accounts, trust documents, advisory agreement, and upcoming tasks (rollover settlement, first statement). The named co-trustee (Walter's brother, successor trustee) receives a scoped view (trust documents only). Cypress's relationship team has the complete multi-account view — no duplicate files, no conflicting versions.

Relationship moves from discovery to ongoing portfolio servicing and trust stewardship without a handoff.

beats.head: The Holcombs and co-trustee access their household Workspace.
beats.lede: Cypress-branded email invites Walter, Esther, and the co-trustee (Walter's brother) into the household Workspace. Walter and Esther see all three accounts; the co-trustee sees only the trust documents and trust-related tasks. Summary shows: IRA (rollover pending settlement), Joint Taxable (open, ready for funding), Family Trust (executed, entity formed).

beats.head: Account states and tasks in one shared view.
beats.lede: Overview shows all three accounts, combined AUM, fee structure (0.75%), and fiduciary relationship status. Tasks show: rollover settlement pending (5-10 business days), first advisory statement (30 days), trust annual reporting (due end of year), and beneficiary review reminder (January anniversary).

beats.head: Iris tracks the household in the agent rail.
beats.lede: Activity shows every account-opening step, document execution, and trust-entity formation. Ask Iris answers questions about fee structure, asset allocation, distributions, trust distributions, and tax treatment without Walter or Esther re-explaining their household structure or goals.

beats.head: Wire authorization and distribution-control tasks open.
beats.lede: For ongoing wealth management, the Workspace surfaces tasks for wire authorizations (tied to Walter and Esther's verified CLEAR identities), distribution requests, and trust amendment procedures. All are executed within the Workspace with full identity binding.

beats.head: Multi-account relationship in motion.
beats.lede: IRA rollover settled, Joint Account funded, Family Trust entity live, all parties in the same Workspace. Cypress's compliance and CLM teams have the complete agreement set from discovery through trust formation. Walter and Esther never wonder about their account status, advisory fee, trust administration, or annual reporting — the Workspace shows what's current, what's coming, and who needs to do what and when.
